title: Enforce sticky routing / single-owner per channel for Topology B
description: Topology B serialization (run_fastapi_mcp) makes a channel's suspended session relocatable across pods, but nothing in-code enforces that only one pod owns a channel at a time. The app workflow domain context persists in embedded RocksDB (speedict), which is single-writer-per-directory: two pods serving the same channel concurrently will lock-conflict/corrupt. Today the code ASSUMES infra (load balancer/router) guarantees one-pod-per-channel. Add an ownership lease/lock (e.g. Redis-based lease keyed by channel_id) so a pod must acquire ownership before serving a channel, with safe handoff on failover/rescale. Note: even if domain state were moved off RocksDB, single-owner is still desirable due to per-process live command-context objects and concurrency control.
acceptance_criteria: A channel cannot be actively served by two pods simultaneously; ownership is acquired/released with safe failover handoff; documented routing contract; integration test simulating contended ownership.
notes: Surfaced during trajectory-serialization review. Related: docs/workflow_execution_context_migration.md (sticky routing section), fastworkflow/workflow.py RocksDB single-writer, fastworkflow/session_state_store.py.

title: Add TTL/reaper for orphaned suspended-session blobs
description: In Topology B, when an agent suspends on ask_user and the user never returns, the durable pending-state blob persists indefinitely. SessionStateStore.save is called on suspend and on LRU eviction (eviction only SAVES, never deletes), and there is no TTL or background reaper. The only cleanup path is the /cancel_pending endpoint (ctx.cancel_pending() + store.clear()). Over time, abandoned blobs accumulate in DiskSessionStateStore (JSON files) and RedisSessionStateStore. Add a TTL/expiry mechanism: e.g. Redis key TTL on save, and a reaper/age-based sweep for the disk backend, with a configurable max-age. Ensure resuming refreshes the TTL.
acceptance_criteria: Abandoned pending blobs are eventually removed without manual cancel_pending; TTL is configurable; resuming an active suspension refreshes its expiry; tests cover expiry on both disk and redis backends.
notes: Surfaced during trajectory-serialization review. Related: fastworkflow/session_state_store.py, fastworkflow/run_fastapi_mcp/utils.py (persist_pending_after_turn, _evict_oldest_if_needed).

title: Add schema migration for serialized session state (schema_version)
description: serialize_state writes schema_version (currently 1) and apply_serialized_state only logs a warning on mismatch; there is no migration logic. If the serialized blob format evolves, existing pending blobs (disk JSON / Redis) will be misread or silently dropped. Add versioned migration: on load, detect schema_version and upgrade older blobs to the current shape (or explicitly discard with a clear policy).
acceptance_criteria: Loading an older-schema blob either migrates it to the current schema or is handled by an explicit documented policy; mismatch no longer silently warns; tests cover an older-version blob.
notes: Surfaced during trajectory-serialization review. Related: fastworkflow/session_state_store.py (SCHEMA_VERSION), fastworkflow/workflow_execution_context.py apply_serialized_state.

title: Restore full command-context navigation depth on rehydration
description: serialize_state captures only current_command_context_name (a string). On cold rehydration, startup is re-run which resets to the root context; if the user had navigated several levels deep into a nested context, that exact position is not reconstructed. apply_serialized_state only logs a debug warning when the rehydrated name differs from the saved one. Design a way to serialize and restore the navigation path/position (e.g. an ordered list of context identifiers + a workflow-specific restore hook) so deep navigation survives relocation.
acceptance_criteria: A session navigated N levels deep resumes at the same context after cold rehydration on a fresh process; covered by an integration test using a nested-context workflow.
notes: Surfaced during trajectory-serialization review. Related: fastworkflow/workflow_execution_context.py serialize_state/apply_serialized_state, tests/test_command_context_serialization_spike.py.
